---
title: "AI / ML Theory Roadmap"
description: "A dependency-aware study path from mathematical foundations to theory-facing machine learning topics."
page-type: roadmap
status: published
update-status: current
level: bridge
topic: ai-ml-theory
prerequisites:
  - /topics/foundation/proofs/index.qmd
  - /topics/foundation/logic/index.qmd
  - /topics/foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd
  - /topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd
  - /topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Choose a sensible math-to-ML theory study order."
  - "Recognize where the roadmap branches into different theory directions."
  - "See which parts of the current site already support the roadmap."
applications:
  - machine-learning-theory
  - research-reading
keywords:
  - roadmap
  - machine learning theory
  - learning theory
  - optimization
  - high-dimensional statistics
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: semiannual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
---

## Purpose

This roadmap is for readers who do not just want to use ML tools, but want to read theory-facing papers, understand assumptions, and know why the main guarantees look the way they do.

It is not a universal ML curriculum. It is a dependency-aware path through the mathematics that most often supports ML theory.

## Who This Is For

Use this roadmap if your goal is any of the following:

- read papers with proofs, bounds, asymptotics, or concentration arguments
- understand why optimization and generalization claims are stated the way they are
- move toward learning theory, high-dimensional statistics, or deep learning theory
- connect the current site's math modules to ML research directions

If your goal is only to get a working model pipeline quickly, this is probably too theory-heavy as a first route.

## Main Sequence

Use this as the default order.

1. [Proofs](../topics/foundation/proofs/index.qmd)
2. [Logic](../topics/foundation/logic/index.qmd)
3. [Linear Algebra](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd)
4. [Probability](../topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd)
5. [Statistics](../topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd)
6. [Single-Variable Calculus](../topics/foundation/single-variable-calculus/index.qmd)
7. [Multivariable Calculus](../topics/foundation/multivariable-calculus/index.qmd)
8. [Optimization](../topics/advanced/optimization/index.qmd)
9. [Real Analysis](../topics/advanced/real-analysis/index.qmd)
10. [Learning Theory](../topics/advanced/learning-theory/index.qmd)
11. [Matrix Analysis](../topics/advanced/matrix-analysis/index.qmd)
12. [High-Dimensional Probability](../topics/advanced/high-dimensional-probability/index.qmd)
13. [High-Dimensional Statistics](../topics/advanced/high-dimensional-statistics/index.qmd)

The first thirteen stages are now live on the site through [High-Dimensional Statistics](../topics/advanced/high-dimensional-statistics/index.qmd), with [Matrix Analysis](../topics/advanced/matrix-analysis/index.qmd) and [High-Dimensional Probability](../topics/advanced/high-dimensional-probability/index.qmd) feeding directly into a complete first-pass high-dimensional-statistics module. The site now also has a full [Numerical Methods](../topics/advanced/numerical-methods/index.qmd) module for the computation side of that stack, which is enough to begin reading the cleaner end of ML-facing theory pages.

## Why This Order Works

### Proofs and Logic First

These pages train the habits that later theory papers assume without apology:

- parse assumptions carefully
- expose hidden quantifiers
- translate between prose and symbolic structure
- negate a statement correctly before trying to prove or refute it

### Linear Algebra Before Most ML

ML is full of vectors, projections, low-rank structure, eigenmodes, and learned linear maps.

Without linear algebra, model descriptions become memorized recipes. With it, many architectures reduce to variations on a small number of reusable objects.

### Probability Before Statistics

Theory-facing ML uses probability to talk about randomness, sampling, concentration, conditioning, and asymptotics.

Statistics then turns that language into estimators, validation, uncertainty, and generalization-facing ideas.

## Shared Core Bridge Into ML

Before branching, there is one short shared core:

1. supervised learning setup
2. loss functions and empirical risk
3. train / validation / test logic
4. calibrated confidence and uncertainty
5. regularization and model complexity

Useful shared-core bridge pages are:

- [Supervised Learning, Losses, and Empirical Risk](../applications/machine-learning/supervised-learning-losses-and-empirical-risk.qmd)
- [Optimization for Machine Learning](../applications/machine-learning/optimization-for-machine-learning.qmd)
- [Generalization, Overfitting, and Validation](../applications/machine-learning/generalization-overfitting-and-validation.qmd)
- [Uncertainty Calibration and Predictive Confidence](../applications/machine-learning/uncertainty-calibration-and-predictive-confidence.qmd)
- [Regularization, Implicit Bias, and Model Complexity](../applications/machine-learning/regularization-implicit-bias-and-model-complexity.qmd)

## Branch Points

After the shared core, most readers should branch instead of forcing one long linear path.

### Optimization Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- gradient descent and SGD
- objective design
- regularization
- training dynamics
- convex and nonconvex viewpoints

### Learning Theory Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- generalization guarantees
- bias-variance structure
- stability and capacity ideas
- VC / Rademacher style reasoning

### High-Dimensional Statistics Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- many-features regimes
- overparameterization
- sparsity and shrinkage
- inference and estimation when $p$ is large relative to $n$

### Deep Learning Theory Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- backpropagation and computation graphs
- implicit bias of optimization
- representation learning
- scaling and overparameterized training

Useful bridge pages for this branch are:

- [Backpropagation and Computation Graphs](../applications/machine-learning/backpropagation-and-computation-graphs.qmd)
- [Attention, Softmax, and Weighted Mixtures](../applications/machine-learning/attention-softmax-and-weighted-mixtures.qmd)
- [In-Context Learning and Linearization](../applications/machine-learning/in-context-learning-and-linearization.qmd)
- [Representation Learning and Geometry of Embeddings](../applications/machine-learning/representation-learning-and-geometry-of-embeddings.qmd)
- [Linear Probes and Representation Diagnostics](../applications/machine-learning/linear-probes-and-representation-diagnostics.qmd)

### Graph and Structured Learning Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- graph diffusion and neighborhood averaging
- message-passing neural networks
- molecular, relational, or recommendation graphs
- spectral versus spatial graph design choices

Useful bridge pages for this branch are:

- [Graph Diffusion and Message Passing](../applications/machine-learning/graph-diffusion-and-message-passing.qmd)
- [Oversmoothing, Depth, and Graph Sampling](../applications/machine-learning/oversmoothing-depth-and-graph-sampling.qmd)
- [Graph Rewiring, Homophily, and Heterophily](../applications/machine-learning/graph-rewiring-homophily-and-heterophily.qmd)
- [Long-Range Dependence and Oversquashing in Graphs](../applications/machine-learning/long-range-dependence-and-oversquashing-in-graphs.qmd)

### Kernel and Bayesian Nonparametrics Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- similarity-based nonlinear prediction
- kernel ridge regression
- Gaussian-process regression
- uncertainty-aware function prediction

Useful bridge pages for this branch are:

- [Kernel Methods and Similarity Geometry](../applications/machine-learning/kernel-methods-and-similarity-geometry.qmd)
- [Kernel Ridge and Gaussian-Process Intuition](../applications/machine-learning/kernel-ridge-and-gaussian-process-intuition.qmd)
- [Bayesian Optimization and Surrogate Modeling](../applications/machine-learning/bayesian-optimization-and-surrogate-modeling.qmd)

### Generative Modeling Branch

Use this branch if you care about:

- denoising as a learning objective
- iterative sample generation
- diffusion probabilistic models
- score-based or stochastic-process views of generation

Useful bridge pages for this branch are:

- [Diffusion Models and Denoising](../applications/machine-learning/diffusion-models-and-denoising.qmd)
- [Score Matching and the SDE View of Diffusion](../applications/machine-learning/score-matching-and-the-sde-view-of-diffusion.qmd)
- [Flow Matching and Transport Views of Generation](../applications/machine-learning/flow-matching-and-transport-views-of-generation.qmd)

## Pages On The Site That Already Support This Roadmap

### Strongest Current Math Support

- [Proofs](../topics/foundation/proofs/index.qmd)
- [Logic](../topics/foundation/logic/index.qmd)
- [Linear Algebra](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/index.qmd)
- [Probability](../topics/foundation/probability/index.qmd)
- [Statistics](../topics/foundation/statistics/index.qmd)

### Strongest Current ML Bridge Pages

- [Machine Learning applications hub](../applications/machine-learning/index.qmd)
- [Regularization, Implicit Bias, and Model Complexity](../applications/machine-learning/regularization-implicit-bias-and-model-complexity.qmd)
- [Attention, Softmax, and Weighted Mixtures](../applications/machine-learning/attention-softmax-and-weighted-mixtures.qmd)
- [Kernel Methods and Similarity Geometry](../applications/machine-learning/kernel-methods-and-similarity-geometry.qmd)
- [Kernel Ridge and Gaussian-Process Intuition](../applications/machine-learning/kernel-ridge-and-gaussian-process-intuition.qmd)
- [Bayesian Optimization and Surrogate Modeling](../applications/machine-learning/bayesian-optimization-and-surrogate-modeling.qmd)
- [Uncertainty Calibration and Predictive Confidence](../applications/machine-learning/uncertainty-calibration-and-predictive-confidence.qmd)
- [Graph Diffusion and Message Passing](../applications/machine-learning/graph-diffusion-and-message-passing.qmd)
- [Oversmoothing, Depth, and Graph Sampling](../applications/machine-learning/oversmoothing-depth-and-graph-sampling.qmd)
- [Graph Rewiring, Homophily, and Heterophily](../applications/machine-learning/graph-rewiring-homophily-and-heterophily.qmd)
- [Long-Range Dependence and Oversquashing in Graphs](../applications/machine-learning/long-range-dependence-and-oversquashing-in-graphs.qmd)
- [Representation Learning and Geometry of Embeddings](../applications/machine-learning/representation-learning-and-geometry-of-embeddings.qmd)
- [Linear Probes and Representation Diagnostics](../applications/machine-learning/linear-probes-and-representation-diagnostics.qmd)
- [Diffusion Models and Denoising](../applications/machine-learning/diffusion-models-and-denoising.qmd)
- [Score Matching and the SDE View of Diffusion](../applications/machine-learning/score-matching-and-the-sde-view-of-diffusion.qmd)
- [Flow Matching and Transport Views of Generation](../applications/machine-learning/flow-matching-and-transport-views-of-generation.qmd)
- [In-Context Learning and Linearization](../applications/machine-learning/in-context-learning-and-linearization.qmd)
- [Linear Regression Through Projection](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/linear-regression-through-projection.qmd)
- [PCA Through SVD](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/pca-through-svd.qmd)
- [Learned Linear Projections in Transformers](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/learned-linear-projections-in-transformers.qmd)
- [Vector Mixtures in Embeddings and Attention](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/vector-mixtures-in-embeddings-and-attention.qmd)

## Paper Reading Overlay

Do not wait until the entire roadmap is complete before touching papers.

Run a light paper-reading overlay in parallel:

1. [How to Read a Paper](../paper-lab/how-to-read-a-paper.qmd)
2. one ML-facing application page
3. one matching paper-lab page

A good current sequence is:

1. [Vector Mixtures in Embeddings and Attention](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/applications/vector-mixtures-in-embeddings-and-attention.qmd)
2. [Paper Lab: Attention as Weighted Vector Mixture](../topics/foundation/linear-algebra/paper-lab/attention-as-weighted-vector-mixture.qmd)

## Common Next Theory Directions

After the current foundations, calculus bridge, optimization path, learning-theory spine, [Matrix Analysis](../topics/advanced/matrix-analysis/index.qmd), [High-Dimensional Probability](../topics/advanced/high-dimensional-probability/index.qmd), [High-Dimensional Statistics](../topics/advanced/high-dimensional-statistics/index.qmd), and [Numerical Methods](../topics/advanced/numerical-methods/index.qmd), the next big theory modules with the best payoff are:

- `Information Theory`
- `ODEs and Dynamical Systems`

## Sources and Further Reading

- [CS229: Machine Learning](https://cs229.stanford.edu/index.html) - `First pass` - official current ML course hub with a broad math-aware overview of the field. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [CS 189 Syllabus](https://eecs189.org/sp26/syllabus/) - `First pass` - official Berkeley syllabus showing a modern intro-ML course with clear prerequisite expectations. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [EE364a: Convex Optimization I](https://web.stanford.edu/class/ee364a/index.html) - `Second pass` - official optimization course that naturally supports the next major branch in the roadmap. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Mathematics for Machine Learning](https://mml-book.github.io/) - `Second pass` - strong math bridge for readers moving from foundations toward ML language. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [CS229T / Statistical Learning Theory](https://web.stanford.edu/class/cs229t/) - `Paper bridge` - a compact entry into theory-heavy ML beyond introductory courses. Checked `2026-04-24`.
